player_utterance: John, I've been sitting with something heavy lately — do you have a moment to talk?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, phone forgotten in the other

player_utterance: It's about the sermon this morning — something in it caught in me like a splinter and I haven't been able to shake it.

believer_state_after.posture: standing very still now, paper cup forgotten in his hand, looking at the player more directly

player_utterance: The "we know where he is now" part — I couldn't stop hearing it after, couldn't make it sit right in my chest.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the damp bench now, not minding the wet, hands clasped between his knees

player_utterance: If I asked you right now, in this moment, would you still choose those words — "we know"?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward on the bench, looking past the player toward the candle on the small table

voice_offers.read: He hasn't answered; pin him to the actual claim.
voice_offers.line: What did you mean by it, exactly? 'We know.' Is that the word you'd choose if I asked you again now?

voice_offers.read: He sat down. The bench is wet. Let him arrive.
voice_offers.line: Thanks for sitting with me. I know today's been long for you too.

player_utterance: The word "know" is doing a lot of heavy lifting there — not hope, not trust, but *know*, the strongest claim you can make. Why do you think that word got chosen?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting back now, one hand flat on the damp bench beside him, looking up at the eaves of the house

voice_offers.read: He's silent three times; the question is already inside him.
voice_offers.line: You haven't answered. That silence is doing more work than the sermon did.

voice_offers.read: Pin him to the verb itself, not the feeling.
voice_offers.line: 'Know' — not hope, not trust. You chose the strongest word in the language. Why that one?

voice_response.matched_line: 'Know' — not hope, not trust. You chose the strongest word in the language. Why that one?

player_utterance: There's no shame in sitting with that — you don't have to answer it the way you'd explain it to a congregation. But forty thousand dollars is a heavy thing to carry into a eulogy, isn't it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward again, elbows on knees, the program folded small in his hands

voice_offers.read: He's cornered on his own vocabulary; press the choice itself.
voice_offers.line: Did you choose it, John, or did the liturgy choose it for you?

voice_offers.read: He's looking away, hand on the bench, sore.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer that the way a teacher would.

player_utterance: You haven't said the full thing out loud yet — not really, not even to yourself. I'm not trying to push you somewhere hard; I just notice it's still sitting just behind your words.

believer_state_after.posture: elbows on knees, the Bible still resting on his thigh under one hand, the other hand at his mouth

voice_offers.read: He's folded small; one true question lands now.
voice_offers.line: Did you believe it when you said it, John? Or did you need to?

voice_offers.read: He's already bent; let him breathe before the next cut.
voice_offers.line: I'm not trying to corner you. I just don't think you've said it yet — to yourself.

voice_response.matched_line: I'm not trying to corner you. I just don't think you've said it yet — to yourself.

player_utterance: There were people — and you just let them hang there, unfinished, like they deserve more than that.

believer_state_after.posture: both hands flat on the Bible in his lap, head bowed, not looking up

voice_offers.read: He's already there; silence finishes what words started.
He's bent; one true question lands now.
voice_offers.line: What did you actually believe when you stood at that pulpit, John?

player_utterance: Those three women, the child with one hand — you don't need to make a case out of them. What would you say if they were sitting here, not as evidence, but just as people?

believer_state_after.posture: the Bible set down on the bench beside him, both hands open in his lap, looking not at the player but at the empty space beside the candle

voice_offers.read: He's bowed; one true name finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say their names, John. Out loud. The ones you took the money from.

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; a hand matters more than a question.
voice_offers.line: You can put the Bible down, John. It's alright.

player_utterance: You don't have to make it a performance — just tell me, who were they to you?

believer_state_after.posture: head down, hands open in his lap, no longer reaching for the Bible

voice_offers.read: He's already there. Naming them would be tender, not strategic.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to perform anything. Just — who were they to you?

voice_offers.read: His hands are open. One name breaks the dam.
voice_offers.line: Start with one. Just one name, John.

voice_response.matched_line: You don't have to perform anything. Just — who were they to you?

player_utterance: Just one face — whoever your mind puts in that empty space right now, start there.

believer_state_after.posture: slid forward off the bench, sitting on the wet flagstones now, back against the bench, hands loose on his knees

voice_offers.read: He's broken open; pressing now would shatter what he's offering.
voice_offers.line: Take your time, John.

voice_offers.read: He's finally close; one name will crack him fully.
voice_offers.line: Just one of them, John. Start with whoever you see right now.

voice_response.matched_line: Just one of them, John. Start with whoever you see right now.

player_utterance: Whose face does your mind give him?

believer_state_after.posture: still on the wet stones, back against the bench, head tipped back now against the wood, eyes closed

voice_offers.read: He's on the ground; pressing more may shatter what could mend.
He's almost there; one more nudge brings the name up.
voice_offers.line: Whose face is it, John?

player_utterance: The face you're seeing right now — whose name comes with it?

believer_state_after.posture: forehead resting on his drawn-up knees now, arms loose around his shins, voice muffled against the fabric of his trousers

voice_offers.read: He's already there; pressing now would be cruelty dressed as inquiry.
His eyes are closed because the face is already there.
voice_offers.line: Say it. The face you're seeing right now — say the name.

voice_response.matched_line: Say it. The face you're seeing right now — say the name.

player_utterance: I'm still here, John, whenever you need to come back up.

believer_state_after.posture: still on the stones, head lifting slowly now, looking at the candle rather than the player

voice_offers.read: He's folded into himself; the question has already landed.
voice_offers.line: Whenever you're ready, John. I'm here.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more quiet push lands it.
voice_offers.line: The name, John. Just the name.

player_utterance: That name you just said — can you say it to me instead of to the light?

believer_state_after.posture: turning his head from the candle to the player, very slowly, holding their gaze

voice_offers.read: He's surfacing on his own; don't yank him up.
He's looking at the candle, not me — the name still wants out.
voice_offers.line: The candle isn't who you're seeing. Say his name to me, not to the flame.

player_utterance: Stay right here with me, John Halvorsen.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting against the bench on the wet stones, hands open in his lap, eyes on the player, breathing slowly

voice_offers.read: He's turning toward me; meet him, don't push.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm right here.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me now — the name will come if I hold.
voice_offers.line: Yes. Look at me. Now say it.
